Pride and ambition cause a lot of trouble both in churches and states. The rebels argued against giving the priesthood to Aaron and his family. They had little reason to boast about the people's purity, because the people had often been polluted with sin and were now under God’s displeasure. They falsely accused Moses and Aaron of taking honor for themselves; however, they were called by God to do so. See here, 1. What spirit-levelers are like—those who resist the powers that God has set over them. 2. How they have been unhelpful. Moses sought instruction from God. A wise person’s heart studies to answer and asks for advice from God. Moses showed their privileges as Levites and proved that they were wrong to undervalue those privileges. It will help us avoid envying those above us if we consider how many people are below them.

Moses summoned Dathan and Abiram to bring their complaints, but they would not obey. They made false charges against Moses. Those who deserve the highest praise often receive the harshest criticism. Moses, though the meekest man, became very angry when he saw God being dishonored in him; he could not bear the people destroying themselves. He appealed to God as a witness of his own integrity. He told them to appear with Aaron the next morning at the time of offering the morning incense. Korah agreed to do so. Proud and ambitious men, while trying to advance themselves, often quickly bring about their own downfall.

The same glory of the Lord that placed Aaron in his office initially now confirmed him in it and confounded those who opposed him. Nothing is more frightening to those who are conscious of guilt than seeing God’s glory. See how dangerous it is to associate with sinners. Even though the people had treacherously deserted them, Moses and Aaron proved themselves faithful shepherds of Israel. If others fail in their duty to us, that does not remove our obligation to care for their well-being. Their prayer was a pleading prayer, and it ultimately prevailed.

The seventy elders of Israel attended Moses. It is our duty to support lawful authority when it is opposed. Those who want to avoid destruction with sinners must separate themselves from them. In response to Moses’s prayer, God stirred up the hearts of the congregation to move for their own safety. Grace to separate from evil-doers is one of the things that accompanies salvation. God, in justice, left the rebels to the stubbornness and hardness of their own hearts. Moses, by God's direction, declared that if the rebels died a common death, he would be willing to be called an imposter while all of Israel waited for what would happen. As soon as Moses spoke those words, God caused the earth to open and swallow them all up. The children perished with their parents; although we cannot know how bad they might have been or how good God might have been otherwise, we are sure that Infinite Justice did them no wrong. It was entirely miraculous. God has, when he chooses, strange punishments for those who do evil. It was very significant. Considering how the earth is still burdened by the weight of people’s sins, we should be amazed that it hasn't sunk under its load yet. The ruin of others should serve as a warning to us. If we could hear the cries of those who have gone down into destruction, we would work harder to escape condemnation ourselves.

A fire came from the Lord and consumed the two hundred fifty men who offered incense; Aaron, who stood with them, was preserved alive. God is jealous for the honor of his own institutions and will not allow them to be violated. The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord. The censers are devoted and, as all consecrated things, must be used for God’s glory. This covering of the altar would remind the people of Israel of this event so that others might hear and fear and not act presumptuously. They brought destruction on themselves both in body and soul. Those who break the law and reject the gospel choose and love death.

The earth barely closed before the same sins were committed again, and all these warnings were ignored. They called the rebels “the people of the Lord” and criticized God’s justice. The stubbornness of Israel, despite seeing God's law on Mount Sinai and experiencing his judgments, shows how necessary God’s grace is to change people’s hearts and lives. Love will do what fear cannot. Moses and Aaron interceded with God for mercy, knowing how great the provocation was. Aaron went and burned incense between the living and the dead—not to purify the air but to appease an offended God. As one concerned for the life of every Israelite, Aaron acted quickly. We must respond to evil with good. Observe especially that Aaron was a type of Christ. There is an infection of sin in the world that only the cross and intercession of Jesus Christ can stop and remove. He entered the defiled and dying camp. He stood between the dead and the living; between the eternal Judge and the souls under condemnation. We must have redemption through His blood, which brings forgiveness of sins. We admire Aaron’s willingness to serve: should we not bless and praise the unspeakable grace and love that filled the Savior's heart when he placed himself in our stead and bought us with his life? God has greatly demonstrated his love for us by dying for us while we were still sinners.
